metadata:

not changed: 
we have different pod types in the chart. with different container port settings.
include "common.containerPorts" expects one list of ports
.Values.service.ports

this does not fit to the charts. Would need something like
.Values.data.service.ports

Please suggest if you see another solution.

- .Values.resources
not - .Values.data.resources

This template is already in use. Please move this to separate commit and either change all users or provide compatibility layer (if .dot does not exist skip the labels part)
Why we need this? This template takes only a single argument
hmm... Do we have any other chart that creates a service account?
no way. containers should be immutable. Installing sth at pod startup is a pure evil, not to mention that it will break offline installer.
Why you need to use a generic service instead of service or headless service?
prefix all those templates with common. those names are global so let's avoid conflicts.

You are right, it was still copy and paste from aaf demo-app code snippet.
If you have a code example I'll change this.
This fine with with me, please support a short review cyle and merge, so I don't stuck with elasticsearch patchset.

These kind of changes were done due to a couple of valueable review comments to previous patchsets.
I'm aware about compatibility and I think this implementation considers already compatibility.
If (include "common.labels" .) is used,
$dot gets '.' context by default
$labels is defaulted by an empty dict, 

This design pattern was already used before in a couple of common templates.
So I don't see a need to change usage in other charts, default include remains unchanged.

Please correct me if I'm wrong.
Ack, a left over of a withdrawn enhancement, will bring it back.
It is used in this entire elasticsearch chart. I'm not yet clear if it is required.

you are right, unfortunatly copy and paste from bitnami helm chart.
I'll check how to avoid this.
Do you know if we have already an init-image in nexus which supports 'sysctl'?

This helper is elasticsearch specific. This is the reason to prefix with 'elasticsearch.'
If you see this as common template I would move this to commom/common.

Your view?

I was asked to enable elasticsearch from SDNC template to check the oom-gating deployment.

I would prefer to change this in the upcoming change in SDNC-OOM charts.
I'll commit this to gerrit as soon as common/elasticsearch is available in master.

Yeah sure, take a look here:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/oom/+/102079/

I propose to proceed as follows:
1) Mark this as WIP
2) Extract changes to common templates to a separate commit
3) Submit the commit to review
4) I'll review it and merge asap
5) Rebase and update this review and remove WIP mark
